response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web developer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C on UNIX, Linux kernel source-level analysis, and embedded LAN-switch security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking). The resume shows no Japanese proficiency, only a brief C/C++ stint (~7 months at National Instruments over a decade ago), no kernel modification experience, and no networking-security or switch-product background. Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms, which does not transfer to embedded firmware security development.
strengths: Broad, long software engineering career with strong testing/TDD craftsmanship culture
Some low-level exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang systems, Linux familiarity
Self-directed contributor with open-source projects and teaching/workshop experience
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, a hard requirement for the role
Lacks required embedded networking/security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, kernel modification)
Recent decade of experience is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js), far from C-on-UNIX firmware development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or kernel experience
Solid general engineering and testing background
